75660.subject: What Claude thinks but never says
75660.body:
The Atlas

Автор: GenAI Works


Автор: GenAI Works

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/what-claude-thinks-never-says-genai-works-auqzf?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3Bo%2BhU4PLbRkaOosHmMDAjiQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=2xARGGnPKaAYk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrce8adt~3h-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrce8adt-3h
  
Welcome back! This edition goes inside the models and around them, from a hidden layer of Claude's thinking to the tools and releases moving fast this week.  In today's Atlas Newsletter: What is Claude thinking but not saying? Anthropic found a hidden layer where Claude holds thoughts it never writes down, and built a lens to read them. OpenAI might ship GPT-5.6 this week: the next model is in preview, and Codex builds hint at how a release would land. Fine-tune agents without fine-tuning: Memento lets AI agents learn from experience without ever touching the model weights. Teach your code: a new Claude Code plugin turns any codebase into an interactive map you can click through. PS: Want to collaborate with the biggest GTM engine and distribution moat for AI companies? You can feature your tool with us and reach millions. What is Claude thinking but not saying? Anthropic researchers have found that Claude carries a hidden layer of thought, a small set of internal patterns that hold concepts the model never writes down.  They call it the J-space, and it resembles the part of your own mind you can actually notice. Your brain runs most of its work automatically, controlling your breathing and posture without your awareness. A smaller slice you can reach directly, like an image that pops into your head.  Neuroscientists call that slice consciously accessible and explain it with global workspace theory, where information becomes accessible once it reaches a small shared channel that broadcasts to the rest of the brain. Claude has something similar. Chain of thought is text a model types to itself, while the J-space stays silent inside the model's activations, so Claude can hold a concept in mind without putting it on the page.  It emerged on its own during training. To read it, the team built the Jacobian lens. Reading buggy code, the J-space holds "ERROR." Reading a prompt injection attack, it holds "injection" and "fake."  And it does real work. Ask Claude to silently think of a sport and the lens shows "Soccer" before it answers, and swapping that pattern for "Rugby" makes Claude report rugby. Swap "spider" for "ant" in a riddle about web-spinners and the answer flips from 8 to 6. Swap "France" for "China" once and Claude's answers about the capital, language, continent and currency all change together, because each reads from the same shared representation. T

75659.subject: Metaplanet Hits 43K BTC.
75659.body:
----------
_**Your Weekly Deep Dive into The World of Crypto**_

**July 8, 2026 · ****5 Min Read**

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/dbcc6435-4772-417e-93b3-9047130eec81/image.png?t=1783490549)
Caption: ~**Market Snapshot · As of July 7, 2026, 8:00 PM ET | BTC Price | YTD Return | Fear & Greed | ETF Flows (Weekly)**~

## 📡** THIS WEEK IN CRYPTO**

**GM, Readers!**

A week ago, $58,000 looked like the trapdoor. Then a 57,000+ job miss walked in, and the bears got carried out. $281M in shorts got cremated, ETFs printed their first green day in 10 sessions, and Strategy went from villain to comeback story in a matter of days.

Bitcoin opened the week near $59,523, hit a 21-month low near $58,000 on Wednesday, rebounded around $62,000 on Friday, and climbed above $63,000 by Sunday. Every altcoin manager screamed "rotation." Every macro trader shrugged and said "one print." 

_**Let’s get into it...**_


----------View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/6c53930f-b4d7-4e7d-baee-6a88bb56a6da/image.png?t=1781097813)
Caption: 

----------
# 🐋 THE BIG WHALE MOVES

### **1. Metaplanet Just Passed 43,000 BTC**

Tokyo-listed Metaplanet added 2,823 BTC in Q2 for $170.7M, pushing total holdings to 43,000 BTC (worth about $2.7B). That makes it the world's third-largest corporate BTC holder, behind Strategy ([843,775 BTC](https://www.strategy.com/purchases) as of July 6) and Twenty One Capital ([43,514 BTC](https://bitcointreasuries.net/public-companies/twenty-one-capital) as of July 7).

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/8e6ce9d1-99ca-4ae3-b99f-23626ebec3f0/image.png?t=1783490633)
Follow image link: (https://x.com/Metaplanet/status/2072573143445544973)
Caption: **Source:**[ @Metaplanet](https://x.com/Metaplanet/status/2072573143445544973)

The flywheel is what makes it interesting. Metaplanet's "Bitcoin Income Generation" arm, basically covered calls on its BTC stack,[ printed $10.95M](https://news.bitcoin.com/metaplanet-buys-2823-bitcoins-for-225m-as-treasury-reaches-43000-btc-holdings/) in Q2 revenue. The effective cost per coin: $77K, below their $80K average buy.

_**Translation: while Strategy fixes its mNAV with buybacks, Metaplanet monetizes volatility to lower cost basis. Same trade, different math.**_

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/1d58b14b-5ba0-4d11-9c68-26e77cc9d5a1/image.png?t=1783490680)
Caption: _**Metaplanet BTC holdings by quarter end | Source: Metaplanet disclosures, **__**[BitcoinTreasuries.net](https://BitcoinTreasuries.net)**_

**👉  Source:**[ Metaplanet Total Bitcoin Holdings](https://contents.xj-storage.jp/xcontents/33500/ef8f0fb6/d3f6/44c3/aafb/fbe407834e59/140120260702587030

🧠 AI’s Crypto News Brief: Bitcoin remains volatile as geopolitical tensions pressure prices, but ETF inflows and institutional buying continue supporting the market. Tokenized equities gain momentum with new infrastructure launches, while Paradigm expands into AI with a $1.2B fund. Polymarket prepares its U.S. comeback after years of regulatory scrutiny. 📈 Bitcoin’s ETF comeback is relying on a $79B futures market betting the rebound holds Futures volume is running far ahead of spot demand, making absorption the next test for BTC's recovery. The post Bitcoin’s ETF comeback is relying on a $79B futures market betting the rebound holds appeared first on CryptoSlate. More details 📉 Polymarket bets on U.S. marketing blitz to win back trust after 4-year ban: Report The prediction market's head of U.S. operations says the business is taking steps to legitimize itself after years of legal scrutiny. Read more 📈 Bitcoin slides as Iran ceasefire collapse sees $75 oil on Hormuz blockade threats Bitcoin price pressure took BTC toward the "crucial" $61,000 mark as oil prices soared on the collapse of the US-Iran ceasefire. More details 💲 Crypto Long & Short: With MSTR concerns assuaged, look to traditional signals around BTC In this week's Crypto Long & Short, FalconX’s Gaspar Martin writes that BTC is reaching a market bottom and is poised for a turnaround. Explore further 🤖 Crypto VC Paradigm launches $1.2 billion AI fund as it broadens beyond digital assets: BBG The firm's latest fund backs AI and robotics startups but leadership says it remains committed to crypto investing. More info 🔑 Dinari, tZERO join forces on turnkey platform for tokenized U.S. equities The move comes as firms race to build the infrastructure for blockchain-based stocks and debate how tokenized stocks should work. More details 📈 Bitcoin ETF Inflows Return As Farside Data Shows Institutions Still Buying The Dip Bitcoin’s supply headlines have been loud, but ETF flow data is giving bulls something to point to. Farside’s numbers show a $143 million net inflow day for US spot Bitcoin ETFs, suggesting institutional buyers are still Explore further Stay ahead in crypto. 75656.subject: Europe’s economy is a mess. Its stock markets are a steal
75656.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MzF9AAcAAAAAA7uEMD3xMlrGM92fW83tugFNr-t-vSudI_tXxUjj4UrvounYLjA7YvZ9MmAs4BCoa1NU7ggOM6pFgr2N_eK-SsGJmjnTkQYrvQy8Dg4SFIGIww 

July 8th 2026

 

 
 
 
The Economist Today

 Our best journalism, handpicked each day


 
 
 

 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MzF9AAcAAAAAA7uEMD3y7hxBYBIRloA4aegWbwISQW9cyFdK-CrwwJ3xijnZxTVoIoTEYxXkHwOOijzo7467Y3ymNwDM6sjFzn4y3v_ay2LazM7cA0-9lp0W9A 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MzF9AAcAAAAAA7uEMD3z-2tqCB95gNFVM3upxMYbg8rYw6DwLLEv2wvp8wdDZo2zAWsJkE5Kx28b_faHmWUZZsULBT36zEeEs2ijlPpaId8OVRNmPQQUAzIYbQ 


 


 
 

Bo Franklin

Senior digital editor



Europe’s economy has little going for it. GDP growth is sluggish compared with much of the world, the continent lacks the AI giants of America and China (and data centres to support them), and its energy markets are vulnerable to wars and other disruptions. But, more than anywhere else in the world, Europe’s stock markets are not its economy, 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MzF9AAcAAAAAA7uEMD30xy0WCxONx9kRuKWAFIiWwqnCeu3kMtggwED0stIrHrnvoOmf_KUngw-9a8394u2xC4YtDR5QSbmUB2-7OlFEw6N8M2bxR6nq 
argues Buttonwood . Its shares deserve a lot more affection.


Nigel Farage hopes to weather a parliamentary investigation and reinvigorate the prospects of Reform UK, his party, by calling (and winning) a by-elec
